title: [Bug]: WebSocket e2e script should suppress transient readiness tracebacks
summary: The optional WebSocket e2e script can print a transient nats-py connection-refused traceback while the temporary local NATS server is still starting, even though the script later passes.
observed: A local WebSocket e2e run eventually published, consumed, wrote, and acknowledged all synthetic messages, but the console included an early transient connection-refused traceback from the client readiness loop.
expected: The script should wait for the temporary WebSocket listener to accept loopback TCP connections before invoking nats-py, so successful runs produce clean sanitized output suitable for release evidence.
reproduction: Run scripts/run-websocket-e2e.sh against a machine where the temporary NATS process needs a short startup window before the WebSocket listener is ready.
failing_test: The defect is covered by adding a focused readiness helper test that proves transient connection failures can be retried without surfacing nats-py tracebacks during normal startup.
impact: The runtime behavior is correct, but noisy test evidence can confuse maintainers and make release notes or issue comments harder to review.
delivery_semantics: The defect is limited to optional test-script readiness behavior. It does not change sink writes, DLQ behavior, idempotency, or the commit-then-ACK invariant.
security: The fix keeps output sanitized and avoids printing stack traces that could expose local paths in public release evidence.
acceptance: The WebSocket e2e script waits for the local WebSocket TCP listener before calling nats-py.
Successful WebSocket e2e runs emit only sanitized JSON status lines.
A focused unit test covers the readiness helper behavior without opening real network sockets.
The live WebSocket e2e script passes when nats-server is available locally.
Documentation remains accurate for the clean output shape.
tests: Run python -m pytest tests/unit/test_websocket_harness.py tests/unit/test_nats_connection_options.py tests/unit/test_config.py and the optional scripts/run-websocket-e2e.sh when nats-server is available.
documentation: No user-facing command changes are required. Existing WebSocket testing documentation should continue to show clean sanitized JSON output.
closeout: Close after the release containing the cleaned WebSocket e2e readiness behavior is published. Include sanitized passing live-script output.
